description: Product framing, incident baseline, glossary, and rollout guardrails for bounded compute/browser resource lifecycle work.

userStories.title: Record the compute browser overload incident baseline
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the overload incident captured in a durable report so that future implementation work targets the actual failure mode instead of only image-count symptoms.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a report under docs/reports that records the 2026-05-17 baseline: Docker image count, container count, BuildKit cache size, stopped/OOM containers, Chromium process count, host RAM/swap class, and affected worker names when known
Explain the distinction between Docker image reuse, container reuse, Ralph sessions, browser-use sessions, and Chromium process trees
State that the primary failure class was unbounded runtime state inside retained workers, not merely many tagged Docker images
Link the report from docs/specs/changes/compute-browser-resource-lifecycle/prds/README.md or the change spec
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented incident baseline report at docs/reports/compute-browser-resource-lifecycle-incident-baseline-2026-05-17.md; evidence recorded in IMPLEMENTATION_PROGRESS.md; npm run typecheck passed in Docker worker.

userStories.title: Add resource lifecycle glossary and operating model docs
userStories.description: As a Ralph job author, I want the resource model documented so that prompts and specs use the same terms for images, workers, browser pools, leases, and worktrees.
acceptanceCriteria: Document the canonical resource model: Docker image, compute container, dev worker, worktree, browser pool, browser lease, auth profile lease, Ralph job, and Ralph run
Document which component owns each resource and which command or policy releases it
State that prompt text cannot override hard resource limits, TTLs, browser-pool reaping, or dirty-worker recycling
Link the model from the compute/browser change spec and PRD catalog
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented canonical resource model at docs/project/compute-browser-resource-operating-model.md and glossary terms in GLOSSARY.md; linked from spec and PRD catalog; npm run typecheck passed in Docker worker.

userStories.title: Create rollout checklist for safe resource lifecycle changes
userStories.description: As an engineering lead, I want a rollout checklist so that browser-pool, Docker-limit, and Ralph-cleanup changes can be enabled without deleting debug state or destabilizing the host.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a rollout checklist covering dry-run diagnostics, Docker command construction tests, browser cleanup tests, repeated browser-use stress validation, all-state compute list/reap verification, and Ralph completion cleanup verification
Checklist states that worktree deletion remains explicit and separate from container/browser cleanup
Checklist includes rollback notes for disabling managed browser-pool mode while keeping Docker limits active
Checklist includes real Docker worker validation before enabling automatic timers
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented rollout checklist at docs/project/compute-browser-resource-rollout-checklist.md with dry-run, Docker, browser, Ralph, rollback, and worktree-separation guidance; npm run typecheck passed in Docker worker.

userStories.title: Add resource lifecycle traceability to existing capability docs
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want existing capability docs to reference the new resource lifecycle contract so that implementation agents do not miss cross-cutting requirements.
acceptanceCriteria: Update relevant capability docs to link to the compute-browser resource lifecycle change and summarize their new obligations
Docker compute docs mention resource budgets, all-state list/reap, and Docker hygiene
Browser automation and browser-use lease docs mention managed CDP reuse, stale chrome/chromium cleanup, and auth-slot coordination
Ralph docs mention worker ownership, cleanup policy, retained-resource visibility, and prompt text not overriding policy
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Updated Docker compute, browser automation, browser-use auth lease, and Ralph capability docs with resource lifecycle obligations and links; npm run typecheck passed in Docker worker.
